host: ubu-2 RTX 5070 sm_120 driver 580 (0% util / 5 MiB at fire)
lever: overlap the P.V mma of K-tile j with the QK^T mma of tile j+1 (software-pipeline the COMPUTE, breaking the intra-warp QK^T->reduce->P.V dependency chain R10 found is now the floor). Distinct from R10's cp.async which overlapped only the LOAD.

v1_serialized_load.note: first cut: cp_async_wait<0> + syncthreads to make K[j+1] resident before its QK^T hoist => SERIALIZED the cp.async load (lost R10's dominant load-overlap lever)
v1_serialized_load.verdict: much SLOWER — sacrificing load-overlap for compute-overlap is net-negative (confirms R10 lever-isolation: cp.async load-overlap was dominant)

v2_triple_buffer.note: race-free: triple-buffer K+V (3 slots), keep 2-deep cp.async LOAD overlap (wait_group<1>) AND hoist QK^T(j+1) from the already-prefetched slot before P.V(j). Numeric bit-identical to R10 (rel 5.6-7.1e-4) => correct, no buffer race.
v2_triple_buffer.verdict: marginally SLOWER than R10 at every N

probeB_verdict: FALSIFIED. The compute-pipeline does NOT improve over R10's 1.149x — even the clean triple-buffered version is +2.4..+4.9% SLOWER. The residual is NOT an exposed compute-dependency bubble: with 4 warps * 20 warps/SM the warp scheduler ALREADY interleaves the wmma streams; there is no idle pipeline bubble for the hoisted QK^T(j+1) to fill. The extra __syncthreads + register pressure (93->104) cost more than any hoist gain. The floor is the FUNDAMENTAL low arithmetic intensity of the 16x16 tile at d=64 (4 d-tiles) vs cuBLAS's single-launch big-GEMM TC pipeline utilization.

campaign_best_ratio.note: probe-B did not beat it; remains the campaign capstone ratio. R11 probe-A d=64 measured ~1.23x vs the fair cuBLAS-TC baseline this session (within run-to-run variance of R10's 1.149x).
